panels.description: Scrape queue per entity, channel files pending upload, and pending channel ID resolutions
panels.title: Total queue depth

panels.description: Total async tasks fleet-wide = num_processes * concurrency per scraper
panels.title: Active async tasks by scraper
Uploads per second by entity × mode

panels.description: Per-proxy_network success and failure rate (5m) of RSS feed fetches by the RSS scraper. Use this to spot networks where the RSS path is degrading. Compare with the YouTube scraper's circuit-breaker panel to see whether degradation tripped the limiter.
panels.title: RSS fetch success / failure rate by proxy_network

panels.description: Per-proxy_network success and failure rate (5m) of InnerTube channel.info calls made by the RSS scraper. Use to spot networks where InnerTube channel resolution is degrading.
panels.title: InnerTube Channel call success / failure rate by proxy_network

panels.description: Per-proxy_network success and failure rate (5m) of InnerTube video.info calls made by the RSS scraper. Use to spot networks where InnerTube video resolution is degrading.
panels.title: InnerTube Video call success / failure ratio by proxy_network
YouTube scrape duration p50/p95/p99 by entity

panels.description: Number of yt-dlp extract_info calls currently in the thread pool. Pins near executor ceiling (≈12 per process) when GIL / executor is the bottleneck.
panels.title: yt-dlp extract_info active (thread-pool occupancy)
YouTube API request p95 by api
scrape.exchange API request p95 by method × status_class

panels.description: Per-proxy_ip p95 request duration over a 10m window, split by api (html = RSS feed download, innertube = InnerTube call). Top 10 slowest proxy + api combinations. Use this to identify which proxies are dragging the timeout rate.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le, api, proxy_network) (rate(api_request_duration_seconds_bucket{job=~"$job",instance=~"$instance"}[10m]))))

panels.title: Top 10 slowest proxies (p50 request duration)

panels.description: Non-zero means at least one worker is in failure backoff.
panels.title: Worker sleep (seconds) by scraper
RSS tier SLA — on-time vs overdue rate
RSS: seconds since channel last processed
Bulk uploads — success / failed / missing per second
Watcher — files detected / skipped / enqueued per second

panels.description: youtube:resolving:<id> SETNX claim race outcomes. 'won' should dominate; a persistent 'lost' tail is the wasted work the snapshot model used to produce.
panels.title: Channel resolution claim outcomes (won/lost)

panels.description: youtube:exchange_channels SISMEMBER lookups. 'hit' should dominate once the SET is warm; 'fallback' near zero means Redis is reachable.
panels.title: Exchange-channels SET lookup outcomes (hit/miss/fallback)

panels.description: Per-id outcomes from yt_resolve_channel_ids.py. Only ticks while the operator pre-resolver is running.
panels.title: Pre-resolver outcomes (resolved/already_resolved/lost_claim/unresolvable)
